33		"github.com/yuin/goldmark/text"
34		"github.com/yuin/goldmark/util"
35	)
36	
37	var (
38		tocResultKey = parser.NewContextKey()
39		tocEnableKey = parser.NewContextKey()
40	)
41	
42	type tocTransformer struct {
43		r renderer.Renderer
44	}
45	
46	func (t *tocTransformer) Transform(n *ast.Document, reader text.Reader, pc parser.Context) {
47		if b, ok := pc.Get(tocEnableKey).(bool); !ok || !b {
48			return
49		}
50	
51		var (
52			toc         tableofcontents.Builder
53			tocHeading  = &tableofcontents.Heading{}
54			level       int
55			row         = -1
56			inHeading   bool
57			headingText bytes.Buffer
58		)
59	
60		if ids := pc.IDs().(stringValuesProvider).StringValues(); len(ids) > 0 {
61			toc.SetIdentifiers(ids)
62		}
63	
64		ast.Walk(n, func(n ast.Node, entering bool) (ast.WalkStatus, error) {
65			s := ast.WalkStatus(ast.WalkContinue)
66			if n.Kind() == ast.KindHeading {
67				if inHeading && !entering {
68					tocHeading.Title = sanitizeTOCHeadingTitle(headingText.String())
69					headingText.Reset()
70					toc.AddAt(tocHeading, row, level-1)
71					tocHeading = &tableofcontents.Heading{}
72					inHeading = false
73					return s, nil
74				}
75	
76				inHeading = true
77			}
78	
79			if !(inHeading && entering) {
80				return s, nil
81			}
82	
83			switch n.Kind() {
84			case ast.KindHeading:
85				heading := n.(*ast.Heading)
86				level = heading.Level
87	
88				if level == 1 || row == -1 {
89					row++
90				}
91	
92				id, found := heading.AttributeString("id")
93				if found {
94					tocHeading.ID = string(id.([]byte))
95					tocHeading.Level = level
96				}
97			case
98				ast.KindCodeSpan,
99				ast.KindLink,
100				ast.KindImage,
101				ast.KindEmphasis,
102				strikethroughAst.KindStrikethrough,
103				emojiAst.KindEmoji,
104				extras.KindDelete,
105				extras.KindInsert,
106				extras.KindMark,
107				extras.KindSubscript,
108				extras.KindSuperscript:
109				err := t.r.Render(&headingText, reader.Source(), n)
110				if err != nil {
111					return s, err
112				}
113	
114				return ast.WalkSkipChildren, nil
115			case
116				ast.KindAutoLink,
117				ast.KindRawHTML,
118				ast.KindText,
119				ast.KindString:
120				err := t.r.Render(&headingText, reader.Source(), n)
121				if err != nil {
122					return s, err
123				}
124			}
125	
126			return s, nil
127		})
128	
129		pc.Set(tocResultKey, toc.Build())
130	}
131	
132	type tocExtension struct {
133		options []renderer.Option
134	}
135	
136	func newTocExtension(options []renderer.Option) goldmark.Extender {
137		return &tocExtension{
138			options: options,
139		}
140	}
141	
142	func (e *tocExtension) Extend(m goldmark.Markdown) {
143		r := goldmark.DefaultRenderer()
144		r.AddOptions(e.options...)
145		m.Parser().AddOptions(parser.WithASTTransformers(util.Prioritized(&tocTransformer{
146			r: r,
147		},
148			// This must run after the ID generation (priority 100).
149			110)))
150	}
151	
152	var tocSanitizerPolicy = newTOCSanitizerPolicy()
153	
154	// newTOCSanitizerPolicy returns a bluemonday policy for sanitizing TOC heading
155	// titles against an allowlist of inline HTML elements and attributes,
156	// specifically excluding anchor elements to prevent links within TOC heading
157	// titles.
158	func newTOCSanitizerPolicy() *bluemonday.Policy {
159		p := bluemonday.NewPolicy()
160		p.AllowElements(
161			"abbr", "b", "bdi", "bdo", "br", "cite", "code", "data", "del", "dfn",
162			"em", "i", "ins", "kbd", "mark", "q", "rp", "rt", "ruby", "s", "samp",
163			"small", "span", "strong", "sub", "sup", "time", "u", "var", "wbr",
164		)
165		p.AllowStandardAttributes()
166		p.AllowStyling()
167		p.AllowImages()
168		p.AllowAttrs("cite").OnElements("del", "ins", "q")
169		p.AllowAttrs("datetime").OnElements("del", "ins", "time")
170		p.AllowAttrs("value").OnElements("data")
171		return p
172	}
173	
174	var whiteSpaceRe = regexp.MustCompile(`\s+`)
175	
176	// sanitizeTOCHeadingTitle sanitizes s for use as a TOC heading title.
177	func sanitizeTOCHeadingTitle(s string) string {
178		if strings.IndexByte(s, '<') == -1 {
179			return s
180		}
181	
182		// Sanitize the string.
183		ss := tocSanitizerPolicy.Sanitize(s)
184	
185		// Remove extraneous whitespace.
186		return whiteSpaceRe.ReplaceAllString(strings.TrimSpace(ss), " ")
187	}
188

content.text: I found the TOC transformer renders whole inline container nodes (like emphasis), which can recurse into extension child nodes unknown to the simplified TOC renderer. Next I’ll inspect how renderer options are passed so the fix fits existing design.

content.text: I found the TOC transformer path and the likely failure mode: it renders a whole known container node (like emphasis), which lets Goldmark recurse into unknown extension children. Next I’ll add a focused regression test to reproduce it before changing the implementation.

92	func (r *htmlRenderer) renderPassthroughBlock(w util.BufWriter, src []byte, node ast.Node, entering bool) (ast.WalkStatus, error) {
93		ctx := w.(*render.Context)
94	
95		if entering {
96			return ast.WalkContinue, nil
97		}
98	
99		var (
100			s      string
101			typ    string
102			delims *passthrough.Delimiters
103		)
104	
105		switch nn := node.(type) {
106		case *passthrough.PassthroughInline:
107			s = string(nn.Text(src))
108			typ = "inline"
109			delims = nn.Delimiters
110		case (*passthrough.PassthroughBlock):
111			l := nn.Lines().Len()
112			var buff bytes.Buffer
113			for i := range l {
114				line := nn.Lines().At(i)
115				buff.Write(line.Value(src))
116			}
117			s = buff.String()
118			typ = "block"
119			delims = nn.Delimiters
120		}
121	
122		renderer := ctx.RenderContext().GetRenderer(hooks.PassthroughRendererType, typ)
123		if renderer == nil {
124			// Write the raw content if no renderer is found.
125			ctx.WriteString(s)
126			return ast.WalkContinue, nil
127		}
128	
129		// Inline and block passthroughs share the same ordinal counter.
130		ordinal := ctx.GetAndIncrementOrdinal(passthrough.KindPassthroughBlock)
131	
132		// Trim the delimiters.
133		s = s[len(delims.Open) : len(s)-len(delims.Close)]
134	
135		pctx := passthroughContext{
136			BaseContext:      render.NewBaseContext(ctx, renderer, node, src, ordinal),
137			inner:            s,
138			typ:              typ,
139			AttributesHolder: attributes.New(node.Attributes(), attributes.AttributesOwnerGeneral),
140		}
141	
142		pr := renderer.(hooks.PassthroughRenderer)
143	
144		if err := pr.RenderPassthrough(ctx.RenderContext().Ctx, w, pctx); err != nil {
145			return ast.WalkStop, err
146		}
147	
148		return ast.WalkContinue, nil
149	}
150	
151	type passthroughContext struct {
152		hooks.BaseContext
153	
154		typ   string // inner or block
155		inner string
156	
157		*attributes.AttributesHolder
158	}

161		return p.typ
162	}

476	// ---- Extension and config ----
477	
478	type passthrough struct {
479		InlineDelimiters []Delimiters
480		BlockDelimiters  []Delimiters
481	}
482	
483	// Config configures this extension.
484	type Config struct {
485		InlineDelimiters []Delimiters
486		BlockDelimiters  []Delimiters
487	}
488	
489	func New(c Config) goldmark.Extender {
490		// The parser executes in two phases:
491		//
492		// Phase 1: parse the input with all delimiters treated as inline, and block delimiters
493		// taking precedence over inline delimiters.
494		//
495		// Phase 2: transform the parsed AST to split paragraphs at the point of
496		// inline passthroughs with matching block delimiters.
497		combinedDelimiters := make([]Delimiters, len(c.InlineDelimiters)+len(c.BlockDelimiters))
498		copy(combinedDelimiters, c.BlockDelimiters)
499		copy(combinedDelimiters[len(c.BlockDelimiters):], c.InlineDelimiters)
500		return &passthrough{
501			InlineDelimiters: combinedDelimiters,
502			BlockDelimiters:  c.BlockDelimiters,
503		}
504	}
505	
506	func (e *passthrough) Extend(m goldmark.Markdown) {
507		m.Parser().AddOptions(
508			parser.WithInlineParsers(
509				util.Prioritized(newInlinePassthroughParser(e.InlineDelimiters), 201),
510			),
511			parser.WithASTTransformers(
512				util.Prioritized(newPassthroughInlineTransformer(e.BlockDelimiters), 0),
513			),
514		)
